40	concurrency:
41	  group: swarm-prod-${{ github.ref }}
42	  cancel-in-progress: false
43	
44	jobs:
45	  deploy:
46	    if: inputs.action == 'deploy'
47	    runs-on: blacksmith-2vcpu-ubuntu-2404
48	    environment: production
49	    permissions:
50	      contents: read
51	
52	    steps:
53	      - name: Checkout repository
54	        uses: actions/checkout@v4
55	
56	      - name: Set up Docker Swarm context
57	        uses: ./.github/actions/setup-swarm-context
58	        with:
59	          ssh_key: ${{ secrets.PROD_VM_SSH_KEY }}
60	          ssh_host: ${{ secrets.PROD_VM_SSH_HOST }}
61	          ssh_user: ${{ secrets.PROD_VM_SSH_USER }}
62	
63	      - name: Log in to GHCR
64	        uses: docker/login-action@c94ce9fb468520275223c153574b00df6fe4bcc9 # v3.7.0
65	        with:
66	          registry: ghcr.io
67	          username: ${{ github.actor }}
68	          password: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
69	
70	      - name: Deploy stack
71	        run: |
72	          docker --context prod stack deploy \
73	            --with-registry-auth \
74	            -c infra/docker/docker-compose.prod.yml \
75	            gaia-prod
76	
77	      - name: Wait for convergence
78	        id: converge
79	        run: |
80	          echo "Waiting for gaia-backend and arq_worker to converge (up to 5 minutes)..."
81	          DEADLINE=$(( $(date +%s) + 300 ))
82	
83	          while true; do
84	            # Detect if Swarm auto-rolled back any app service
85	            for svc in gaia-backend arq_worker; do
86	              state=$(docker --context prod service inspect "gaia-prod_${svc}" \
87	                --format '{{.UpdateStatus.State}}' 2>/dev/null || echo "")
88	              if echo "$state" | grep -qE "rollback_completed|rollback_started|rollback_paused"; then
89	                echo "rolled_back=true" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
90	                echo "::error::gaia-prod_${svc} was rolled back by Swarm (state: $state)"
91	                exit 1
92	              fi
93	            done
94	
95	            # Check running replicas for both app services
96	            running_backend=$(docker --context prod service ps gaia-prod_gaia-backend \
97	              --filter desired-state=running \
98	              --format '{{.CurrentState}}' 2>/dev/null | grep -c "^Running" || true)
99	            running_backend=${running_backend:-0}
100	
101	            running_worker=$(docker --context prod service ps gaia-prod_arq_worker \
102	              --filter desired-state=running \
103	              --format '{{.CurrentState}}' 2>/dev/null | grep -c "^Running" || true)
104	            running_worker=${running_worker:-0}
105	
106	            if [ "$running_backend" -ge 1 ] && [ "$running_worker" -ge 1 ]; then
107	              echo "gaia-backend and arq_worker are running"
108	              break
109	            fi
110	
111	            now=$(date +%s)
112	            if [ "$now" -ge "$DEADLINE" ]; then
113	              echo "timed_out=true" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
114	              echo "::error::services did not converge within 5 minutes (backend=${running_backend} worker=${running_worker})"
115	              exit 1
116	            fi
117	
118	            remaining=$(( DEADLINE - now ))
119	            echo "  ... waiting (backend=${running_backend} worker=${running_worker}, ${remaining}s remaining)"
120	            sleep 15
121	          done
122	
123	      - name: Push deploy annotation to Loki
124	        if: success()
125	        env:
126	          LOKI_PUSH_URL: ${{ secrets.LOKI_PUSH_URL }}
127	        run: |
128	          [ -z "$LOKI_PUSH_URL" ] && exit 0
129	          TS_NS="$(date +%s%N)"
130	          LINE=$(printf '{"event":"deploy","service":"api","sha":"%s","actor":"%s","ref":"%s"}' \
131	            "${{ github.sha }}" "${{ github.actor }}" "${{ github.ref_name }}")
132	          PAYLOAD=$(jq -n \
133	            --arg ts "$TS_NS" \
134	            --arg line "$LINE" \
135	            '{"streams":[{"stream":{"event":"deploy","service":"ci","source":"github_actions"},"values":[[$ts,$line]]}]}')
136	          curl -sS -X POST "$LOKI_PUSH_URL" \
137	            -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
138	            -d "$PAYLOAD"
139	
140	      - name: Notify Discord — deploy succeeded
141	        if: success()
142	        uses: appleboy/discord-action@afc227330777ad298156a1f5233dfa1e885616db # v1.2.0
143	        with:
144	          webhook_url: ${{ secrets.DISCORD_WEBHOOK }}
145	          username: "Gaia Deploy Bot"
146	          color: "#48f442"
147	          message: |
148	            **Backend Deploy Succeeded**
149	            **Commit:** `${{ github.sha }}`
150	            **Branch:** ${{ github.ref_name }}
151	            **Actor:** ${{ github.actor }}
152	
153	      - name: Notify Discord — Swarm auto-rollback
154	        if: failure() && steps.converge.outputs.rolled_back == 'true'
155	        uses: appleboy/discord-action@afc227330777ad298156a1f5233dfa1e885616db # v1.2.0
156	        with:
157	          webhook_url: ${{ secrets.DISCORD_WEBHOOK }}
158	          username: "Gaia Deploy Bot"
159	          color: "#ffa500"
160	          message: |
161	            **Backend Deploy Failed — Swarm Auto-Rolled Back**
162	            The new image failed health checks and Swarm reverted to the previous revision.
163	            **Commit:** `${{ github.sha }}`
164	            **Branch:** ${{ github.ref_name }}
165	            **Actor:** ${{ github.actor }}
166	
167	      - name: Notify Discord — convergence timeout
168	        if: failure() && steps.converge.outputs.timed_out == 'true'
169	        uses: appleboy/discord-action@afc227330777ad298156a1f5233dfa1e885616db # v1.2.0
170	        with:
171	          webhook_url: ${{ secrets.DISCORD_WEBHOOK }}
172	          username: "Gaia Deploy Bot"
173	          color: "#ff6b6b"
174	          message: |
175	            **Backend Deploy Failed — Convergence Timeout**
176	            gaia-backend and arq_worker did not reach Running state within 5 minutes. Check `docker service ps gaia-prod_gaia-backend` and `docker service ps gaia-prod_arq_worker` on the VM.
177	            **Commit:** `${{ github.sha }}`
178	            **Branch:** ${{ github.ref_name }}
179	            **Actor:** ${{ github.actor }}
180	
181	      - name: Notify Discord — deploy failed
182	        if: failure() && steps.converge.outputs.rolled_back != 'true' && steps.converge.outputs.timed_out != 'true'
183	        uses: appleboy/discord-action@afc227330777ad298156a1f5233dfa1e885616db # v1.2.0
184	        with:
185	          webhook_url: ${{ secrets.DISCORD_WEBHOOK }}
186	          username: "Gaia Deploy Bot"
187	          color: "#ff6b6b"
188	          message: |
189	            **Backend Deploy Failed**
190	            The deploy step failed before Swarm could start convergence (image pull, auth, or config error).
191	            **Commit:** `${{ github.sha }}`
192	            **Branch:** ${{ github.ref_name }}
193	            **Actor:** ${{ github.actor }}
194	
195	  rollback:
196	    if: github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch' && inputs.action == 'rollback'
197	    runs-on: blacksmith-2vcpu-ubuntu-2404
198	    environment: production
199	    permissions:
200	      contents: read
201	
202	    steps:
203	      - name: Checkout repository
204	        uses: actions/checkout@v4
205	
206	      - name: Set up Docker Swarm context
207	        uses: ./.github/actions/setup-swarm-context
208	        with:

210	          ssh_host: ${{ secrets.PROD_VM_SSH_HOST }}
211	          ssh_user: ${{ secrets.PROD_VM_SSH_USER }}
212	
213	      - name: Log in to GHCR
214	        uses: docker/login-action@c94ce9fb468520275223c153574b00df6fe4bcc9 # v3.7.0
215	        with:
216	          registry: ghcr.io
217	          username: ${{ github.actor }}
218	          password: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
219	
220	      - name: Roll back app stack
221	        env:
222	          ROLLBACK_MODE: ${{ inputs.rollback_mode }}
223	          IMAGE_DIGEST: ${{ inputs.image_digest }}
224	        run: |
225	          if [ "$ROLLBACK_MODE" = "last" ]; then
226	            for svc in $(docker --context prod stack services gaia-prod --format '{{.Name}}' \
227	              | grep -E 'gaia-backend|arq_worker|discord-bot|slack-bot|telegram-bot|whatsapp-bot'); do
228	              echo "Rolling back $svc..."
229	              docker --context prod service rollback "$svc"
230	            done
231	          elif [ "$ROLLBACK_MODE" = "digest" ]; then
232	            [ -z "$IMAGE_DIGEST" ] && echo "image_digest required for digest mode" && exit 1
233	            docker --context prod service update --image "$IMAGE_DIGEST" gaia-prod_gaia-backend
234	            docker --context prod service update --image "$IMAGE_DIGEST" gaia-prod_arq_worker
235	          fi
236	
237	      - name: Wait for rollback convergence
238	        id: converge
239	        env:
240	          ROLLBACK_MODE: ${{ inputs.rollback_mode }}
241	        run: |
242	          if [ "$ROLLBACK_MODE" = "last" ]; then
243	            SERVICES=$(docker --context prod stack services gaia-prod --format '{{.Name}}' \
244	              | grep -E 'gaia-backend|arq_worker|discord-bot|slack-bot|telegram-bot|whatsapp-bot')
245	          else
246	            SERVICES="gaia-prod_gaia-backend gaia-prod_arq_worker"
247	          fi
248	          echo "Waiting for services to stabilise (up to 3 minutes): $SERVICES"
249	          DEADLINE=$(( $(date +%s) + 180 ))
250	          while true; do
251	            pending=()
252	            for svc in $SERVICES; do
253	              running=$(docker --context prod service ps "$svc" \
254	                --filter desired-state=running \
255	                --format '{{.CurrentState}}' 2>/dev/null | grep -c "^Running" || true)
256	              [ "${running:-0}" -lt 1 ] && pending+=("$svc")
257	            done
258	            if [ "${#pending[@]}" -eq 0 ]; then
259	              echo "All services are running"
260	              break
261	            fi
262	            if [ "$(date +%s)" -ge "$DEADLINE" ]; then
263	              echo "timed_out=true" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
264	              echo "::warning::Did not stabilise within 3 minutes: ${pending[*]}"
265	              break  # don't fail — rollback itself succeeded
266	            fi
267	            sleep 10
268	          done
269	
270	      - name: Push rollback annotation to Loki
271	        if: success()
272	        env:
273	          LOKI_PUSH_URL: ${{ secrets.LOKI_PUSH_URL }}
274	          ROLLBACK_MODE: ${{ inputs.rollback_mode }}
275	          ACTOR: ${{ github.actor }}
276	          REF_NAME: ${{ github.ref_name }}
277	        run: |
278	          [ -z "$LOKI_PUSH_URL" ] && exit 0
279	          TS_NS="$(date +%s%N)"
280	          LINE=$(printf '{"event":"rollback","mode":"%s","actor":"%s","ref":"%s"}' \
281	            "$ROLLBACK_MODE" "$ACTOR" "$REF_NAME")
282	          PAYLOAD=$(jq -n \
283	            --arg ts "$TS_NS" \
284	            --arg line "$LINE" \
285	            '{"streams":[{"stream":{"event":"rollback","service":"ci","source":"github_actions"},"values":[[$ts,$line]]}]}')
286	          curl -sS -X POST "$LOKI_PUSH_URL" \
287	            -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
288	            -d "$PAYLOAD"
289	
290	      - name: Notify Discord — rollback succeeded
291	        if: success()
292	        uses: appleboy/discord-action@afc227330777ad298156a1f5233dfa1e885616db # v1.2.0
293	        with:
294	          webhook_url: ${{ secrets.DISCORD_WEBHOOK }}
295	          username: "Gaia Deploy Bot"
296	          color: "#ffa500"
297	          message: |
298	            **Backend Rollback Succeeded**
299	            **Mode:** ${{ inputs.rollback_mode }}${{ inputs.image_digest && format(' (`{0}`)', inputs.image_digest) || '' }}
300	            **Actor:** ${{ github.actor }}
301	            ${{ steps.converge.outputs.timed_out == 'true' && '⚠️ Note: not all services stabilised within 3 minutes — verify manually.' || '' }}
302	
303	      - name: Notify Discord — rollback failed
304	        if: failure()
305	        uses: appleboy/discord-action@afc227330777ad298156a1f5233dfa1e885616db # v1.2.0
306	        with:
307	          webhook_url: ${{ secrets.DISCORD_WEBHOOK }}
308	          username: "Gaia Deploy Bot"
309	          color: "#ff6b6b"
310	          message: |
311	            **Backend Rollback Failed**
312	            **Mode:** ${{ inputs.rollback_mode }}
313	            **Actor:** ${{ github.actor }}
314	            The rollback command itself failed. Production state is unknown — check the VM immediately.

file.content:
name: Deploy Swarm Production

on:
  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      action:
        description: "Operation to run"
        required: true
        default: deploy
        type: choice
        options:
          - deploy
          - rollback
      rollback_mode:
        description: "Rollback mode (rollback only)"
        required: false
        default: last
        type: choice
        options:
          - last
          - digest
      image_digest:
        description: "Full image ref for digest rollback (e.g. ghcr.io/org/gaia@sha256:...)"
        required: false
        type: string
  workflow_call:
    inputs:
      action:
        required: false
        type: string
        default: deploy
      rollback_mode:
        required: false
        type: string
        default: last
      image_digest:
        required: false
        type: string

concurrency:
  group: swarm-prod-${{ github.ref }}
  cancel-in-progress: false

jobs:
  deploy:
    if: inputs.action == 'deploy'
    runs-on: blacksmith-2vcpu-ubuntu-2404
    environment: production
    permissions:
      contents: read

    steps:
      - name: Checkout repository
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Set up Docker Swarm context
        uses: ./.github/actions/setup-swarm-context
        with:
          ssh_key: ${{ secrets.PROD_VM_SSH_KEY }}
          ssh_host: ${{ secrets.PROD_VM_SSH_HOST }}
          ssh_user: ${{ secrets.PROD_VM_SSH_USER }}

      - name: Log in to GHCR
        uses: docker/login-action@c94ce9fb468520275223c153574b00df6fe4bcc9 # v3.7.0
        with:
          registry: ghcr.io
          username: ${{ github.actor }}
          password: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}

      - name: Deploy stack
        run: |
          docker --context prod stack deploy \
            --with-registry-auth \
            -c infra/docker/docker-compose.prod.yml \
            gaia-prod

      - name: Wait for convergence
        id: converge
        run: |
          echo "Waiting for gaia-backend and arq_worker to converge (up to 5 minutes)..."
          DEADLINE=$(( $(date +%s) + 300 ))

          while true; do
            # Detect if Swarm auto-rolled back any app service
            for svc in gaia-backend arq_worker; do
              state=$(docker --context prod service inspect "gaia-prod_${svc}" \
                --format '{{.UpdateStatus.State}}' 2>/dev/null || echo "")
              if echo "$state" | grep -qE "rollback_completed|rollback_started|rollback_paused"; then
                echo "rolled_back=true" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
                echo "::error::gaia-prod_${svc} was rolled back by Swarm (state: $state)"
                exit 1
              fi
            done

            # Check running replicas for both app services
            running_backend=$(docker --context prod service ps gaia-prod_gaia-backend \
              --filter desired-state=running \
              --format '{{.CurrentState}}' 2>/dev/null | grep -c "^Running" || true)
            running_backend=${running_backend:-0}

            running_worker=$(docker --context prod service ps gaia-prod_arq_worker \
              --filter desired-state=running \
              --format '{{.CurrentState}}' 2>/dev/null | grep -c "^Running" || true)
            running_worker=${running_worker:-0}

            if [ "$running_backend" -ge 1 ] && [ "$running_worker" -ge 1 ]; then
              echo "gaia-backend and arq_worker are running"
              break
            fi

            now=$(date +%s)
            if [ "$now" -ge "$DEADLINE" ]; then
              echo "timed_out=true" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
              echo "::error::services did not converge within 5 minutes (backend=${running_backend} worker=${running_worker})"
              exit 1
            fi

            remaining=$(( DEADLINE - now ))
            echo "  ... waiting (backend=${running_backend} worker=${running_worker}, ${remaining}s remaining)"
            sleep 15
          done

      - name: Push deploy annotation to Loki
        if: success()
        env:
          LOKI_PUSH_URL: ${{ secrets.LOKI_PUSH_URL }}
        run: |
          [ -z "$LOKI_PUSH_URL" ] && exit 0
          TS_NS="$(date +%s%N)"
          LINE=$(printf '{"event":"deploy","service":"api","sha":"%s","actor":"%s","ref":"%s"}' \
            "${{ github.sha }}" "${{ github.actor }}" "${{ github.ref_name }}")
          PAYLOAD=$(jq -n \
            --arg ts "$TS_NS" \
            --arg line "$LINE" \
            '{"streams":[{"stream":{"event":"deploy","service":"ci","source":"github_actions"},"values":[[$ts,$line]]}]}')
          curl -sS -X POST "$LOKI_PUSH_URL" \
            -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
            -d "$PAYLOAD"

      - name: Notify Discord — deploy succeeded
        if: success()
        uses: appleboy/discord-action@afc227330777ad298156a1f5233dfa1e885616db # v1.2.0
        with:
          webhook_url: ${{ secrets.DISCORD_WEBHOOK }}
          username: "Gaia Deploy Bot"
          color: "#48f442"
          message: |
            **Backend Deploy Succeeded**
            **Commit:** `${{ github.sha }}`
            **Branch:** ${{ github.ref_name }}
            **Actor:** ${{ github.actor }}

      - name: Notify Discord — Swarm auto-rollback
        if: failure() && steps.converge.outputs.rolled_back == 'true'
        uses: appleboy/discord-action@afc227330777ad298156a1f5233dfa1e885616db # v1.2.0
        with:
          webhook_url: ${{ secrets.DISCORD_WEBHOOK }}
          username: "Gaia Deploy Bot"
          color: "#ffa500"
          message: |
            **Backend Deploy Failed — Swarm Auto-Rolled Back**
            The new image failed health checks and Swarm reverted to the previous revision.
            **Commit:** `${{ github.sha }}`
            **Branch:** ${{ github.ref_name }}
            **Actor:** ${{ github.actor }}

      - name: Notify Discord — convergence timeout
        if: failure() && steps.converge.outputs.timed_out == 'true'
        uses: appleboy/discord-action@afc227330777ad298156a1f5233dfa1e885616db # v1.2.0
        with:
          webhook_url: ${{ secrets.DISCORD_WEBHOOK }}
          username: "Gaia Deploy Bot"
          color: "#ff6b6b"
          message: |
            **Backend Deploy Failed — Convergence Timeout**
            gaia-backend and arq_worker did not reach Running state within 5 minutes. Check `docker service ps gaia-prod_gaia-backend` and `docker service ps gaia-prod_arq_worker` on the VM.
            **Commit:** `${{ github.sha }}`
            **Branch:** ${{ github.ref_name }}
            **Actor:** ${{ github.actor }}

      - name: Notify Discord — deploy failed
        if: failure() && steps.converge.outputs.rolled_back != 'true' && steps.converge.outputs.timed_out != 'true'
        uses: appleboy/discord-action@afc227330777ad298156a1f5233dfa1e885616db # v1.2.0
        with:
          webhook_url: ${{ secrets.DISCORD_WEBHOOK }}
          username: "Gaia Deploy Bot"
          color: "#ff6b6b"
          message: |
            **Backend Deploy Failed**
            The deploy step failed before Swarm could start convergence (image pull, auth, or config error).
            **Commit:** `${{ github.sha }}`
            **Branch:** ${{ github.ref_name }}
            **Actor:** ${{ github.actor }}

  rollback:
    if: github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch' && inputs.action == 'rollback'
    runs-on: blacksmith-2vcpu-ubuntu-2404
    environment: production
    permissions:
      contents: read

    steps:
      - name: Checkout repository
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Set up Docker Swarm context
        uses: ./.github/actions/setup-swarm-context
        with:
          ssh_key: ${{ secrets.PROD_VM_SSH_KEY }}
          ssh_host: ${{ secrets.PROD_VM_SSH_HOST }}
          ssh_user: ${{ secrets.PROD_VM_SSH_USER }}

      - name: Log in to GHCR
        uses: docker/login-action@c94ce9fb468520275223c153574b00df6fe4bcc9 # v3.7.0
        with:
          registry: ghcr.io
          username: ${{ github.actor }}
          password: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}

      - name: Roll back app stack
        env:
          ROLLBACK_MODE: ${{ inputs.rollback_mode }}
          IMAGE_DIGEST: ${{ inputs.image_digest }}
        run: |
          if [ "$ROLLBACK_MODE" = "last" ]; then
            for svc in $(docker --context prod stack services gaia-prod --format '{{.Name}}' \
              | grep -E 'gaia-backend|arq_worker|discord-bot|slack-bot|telegram-bot|whatsapp-bot'); do
              echo "Rolling back $svc..."
              docker --context prod service rollback "$svc"
            done
          elif [ "$ROLLBACK_MODE" = "digest" ]; then
            [ -z "$IMAGE_DIGEST" ] && echo "image_digest required for digest mode" && exit 1
            docker --context prod service update --image "$IMAGE_DIGEST" gaia-prod_gaia-backend
            docker --context prod service update --image "$IMAGE_DIGEST" gaia-prod_arq_worker
          fi

      - name: Wait for rollback convergence
        id: converge
        env:
          ROLLBACK_MODE: ${{ inputs.rollback_mode }}
        run: |
          if [ "$ROLLBACK_MODE" = "last" ]; then
            SERVICES=$(docker --context prod stack services gaia-prod --format '{{.Name}}' \
              | grep -E 'gaia-backend|arq_worker|discord-bot|slack-bot|telegram-bot|whatsapp-bot')
          else
            SERVICES="gaia-prod_gaia-backend gaia-prod_arq_worker"
          fi
          echo "Waiting for services to stabilise (up to 3 minutes): $SERVICES"
          DEADLINE=$(( $(date +%s) + 180 ))
          while true; do
            pending=()
            for svc in $SERVICES; do
              running=$(docker --context prod service ps "$svc" \
                --filter desired-state=running \
                --format '{{.CurrentState}}' 2>/dev/null | grep -c "^Running" || true)
              [ "${running:-0}" -lt 1 ] && pending+=("$svc")
            done
            if [ "${#pending[@]}" -eq 0 ]; then
              echo "All services are running"
              break
            fi
            if [ "$(date +%s)" -ge "$DEADLINE" ]; then
              echo "timed_out=true" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
              echo "::warning::Did not stabilise within 3 minutes: ${pending[*]}"
              break  # don't fail — rollback itself succeeded
            fi
            sleep 10
          done

      - name: Push rollback annotation to Loki
        if: success()
        env:
          LOKI_PUSH_URL: ${{ secrets.LOKI_PUSH_URL }}
          ROLLBACK_MODE: ${{ inputs.rollback_mode }}
          ACTOR: ${{ github.actor }}
          REF_NAME: ${{ github.ref_name }}
        run: |
          [ -z "$LOKI_PUSH_URL" ] && exit 0
          TS_NS="$(date +%s%N)"
          LINE=$(printf '{"event":"rollback","mode":"%s","actor":"%s","ref":"%s"}' \
            "$ROLLBACK_MODE" "$ACTOR" "$REF_NAME")
          PAYLOAD=$(jq -n \
            --arg ts "$TS_NS" \
            --arg line "$LINE" \
            '{"streams":[{"stream":{"event":"rollback","service":"ci","source":"github_actions"},"values":[[$ts,$line]]}]}')
          curl -sS -X POST "$LOKI_PUSH_URL" \
            -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
            -d "$PAYLOAD"

      - name: Notify Discord — rollback succeeded
        if: success()
        uses: appleboy/discord-action@afc227330777ad298156a1f5233dfa1e885616db # v1.2.0
        with:
          webhook_url: ${{ secrets.DISCORD_WEBHOOK }}
          username: "Gaia Deploy Bot"
          color: "#ffa500"
          message: |
            **Backend Rollback Succeeded**
            **Mode:** ${{ inputs.rollback_mode }}${{ inputs.image_digest && format(' (`{0}`)', inputs.image_digest) || '' }}
            **Actor:** ${{ github.actor }}
            ${{ steps.converge.outputs.timed_out == 'true' && '⚠️ Note: not all services stabilised within 3 minutes — verify manually.' || '' }}

      - name: Notify Discord — rollback failed
        if: failure()
        uses: appleboy/discord-action@afc227330777ad298156a1f5233dfa1e885616db # v1.2.0
        with:
          webhook_url: ${{ secrets.DISCORD_WEBHOOK }}
          username: "Gaia Deploy Bot"
          color: "#ff6b6b"
          message: |
            **Backend Rollback Failed**
            **Mode:** ${{ inputs.rollback_mode }}
            **Actor:** ${{ github.actor }}
            The rollback command itself failed. Production state is unknown — check the VM immediately.

zoxide: detected a possible configuration issue.
Please ensure that zoxide is initialized right at the end of your shell configuration file (usually ~/.zshrc).

---DEPLOYMENT_PLAN---
        run: echo "Web not affected, skipping Docker build"

  deployment-plan:
    # Keep deploy planning behind both build lanes so trigger jobs never race ahead.
    needs: [docker-release, docker-web]
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    outputs:
      backend_deploy: ${{ steps.plan.outputs.backend_deploy }}
      frontend_deploy: ${{ steps.plan.outputs.frontend_deploy }}
    steps:
      # Single decision point that translates backend affected + manual mode into deploy booleans.
      - name: Compute deploy plan
        id: plan
        env:
          REF: ${{ github.ref }}
          EVENT_NAME: ${{ github.event_name }}
          MANUAL_MODE_INPUT: ${{ inputs.deployment_mode || '' }}
          MANUAL_MODE_EVENT: ${{ github.event.inputs.deployment_mode || '' }}
          API_AFFECTED: ${{ needs.docker-release.outputs.api_affected || 'false' }}
          BOTS_AFFECTED: ${{ needs.docker-release.outputs.bots_affected || 'false' }}
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail

          backend_deploy=false
          frontend_deploy=false
          manual_mode="${MANUAL_MODE_INPUT:-}"

          if [ -z "$manual_mode" ]; then
            manual_mode="${MANUAL_MODE_EVENT:-}"
          fi
          if [ -z "$manual_mode" ]; then
            manual_mode="auto"
          fi

          if [ "$REF" != "refs/heads/master" ]; then
            echo "Not on master; deploy jobs disabled."
          elif [ "$EVENT_NAME" = "workflow_dispatch" ]; then
            case "$manual_mode" in
              auto)
                if [ "$API_AFFECTED" = "true" ] || [ "$BOTS_AFFECTED" = "true" ]; then
                  backend_deploy=true
                fi
                # Frontend sync to Vercel source repo is always allowed on master.
                frontend_deploy=true
                ;;
              backend-only)
                backend_deploy=true
                ;;
              frontend-only)
                frontend_deploy=true
                ;;
              both)
                backend_deploy=true
                frontend_deploy=true
                ;;
              none)
                ;;
              *)
                echo "::error::Invalid deployment_mode '$manual_mode'. Use auto, backend-only, frontend-only, both, or none."
                exit 1

content.text:
OK, now I have the real picture. Here it is, clean:

## What happens when you merge PR #681 (to develop)

**Nothing automatic deploys.** Develop is just an integration branch.

## What deploys to prod

`deploy-swarm-prod.yml` is what runs the swarm deploy:
- SSHes to the Hetzner VM (`PROD_VM_SSH_HOST` secret)
- Runs `docker --context prod stack deploy -c infra/docker/docker-compose.prod.yml gaia-prod`
- Waits up to 5 min for `gaia-backend` + `arq_worker` to converge
- Notifies Discord

**Trigger paths:**
1. **Manual** — GitHub Actions → "Deploy Swarm Production" → Run workflow → `action: deploy`
2. **From `build.yml`** — but only if `REF == refs/heads/master` AND `backend_deploy == true` (which requires `API_AFFECTED` or `BOTS_AFFECTED`). Just a compose-file change doesn't mark the API as affected.

## So the actual sequence for this fix

1. ✅ PR #681 → develop (done, you merge)
2. Open a develop → master PR (or whatever your release flow is), merge to master
3. **Manually trigger** `Deploy Swarm Production` workflow on `master` branch with `action: deploy`
   - This is the cleanest path because the `build.yml` auto-deploy path may not fire for a pure infra change (`API_AFFECTED` would be false)
4. The workflow does `docker stack deploy` with the new compose
5. Swarm rolls postgres → new container starts → named `pgdata` volume is empty → pg18 inits fresh empty cluster → **previous ~30 MB of active langgraph data is now orphaned** (in the old anonymous volume on the host, still recoverable for a while)
6. Restore the pre-deploy `pg_dumpall` you took, OR accept the data loss since users are minimal

## Critical timing point

Between step 4 (deploy starts) and the moment you restore the dump, prod postgres is **empty**. The API will start writing fresh state into the empty `langgraph` DB. If you don't restore quickly, those minutes of writes are "real" data that's now in the new (correct) volume, mixed with nothing else.

**Recommended order:**
1. Right before triggering deploy: `pg_dumpall` from the live container, save locally
2. Trigger deploy via GH Actions
3. As soon as workflow converges (Discord ping), restore the dump

If you want, I can also add a small `pre-deploy-backup.yml` workflow that auto-takes a dump and uploads it as a workflow artifact before any swarm deploy — would be a one-time addition to make this safer permanently. Want that as a follow-up PR?

aiTitle: Fix PostgreSQL Docker volume configuration for version 18

content.text:
Orphan dump completed (5.9GB SQL, SSH reset at end is harmless — file content intact). Sidecar stopped.

Now tightening the PR description:

input.command:
gh pr edit 681 --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## Summary

The `postgres:18-alpine` image changed its data layout to `/var/lib/postgresql/<major>/docker/` (Debian `pg_ctlcluster` convention). Our compose files mount the named `pgdata` volume at the legacy `/var/lib/postgresql/data` path, which means the actual data directory ends up **outside** the named volume — Docker silently creates an anonymous volume to back the new path on every container start.

**Effect:** every container recreation orphans the previous anonymous volume and spawns a new empty one. The named `pgdata` volume stays empty. Cluster data does not persist across restarts.

Introduced by the pg18 bump in cd47526b1.

## Fix

```diff
volumes:
-  - pgdata:/var/lib/postgresql/data
+  - pgdata:/var/lib/postgresql
```

Applied identically to:
- `infra/docker/docker-compose.yml`
- `infra/docker/docker-compose.prod.yml`
- `infra/docker/docker-compose.selfhost.yml`

With this change, the named `pgdata` volume covers the path postgres 18 actually writes to, so writes persist as intended.

## Test plan

- [ ] Local: `docker compose -f infra/docker/docker-compose.yml down -v && docker compose -f infra/docker/docker-compose.yml up -d` → postgres starts cleanly, named `pgdata` volume contains `18/docker/` subdir after startup
- [ ] Local: restart the container, verify cluster data still present in `pgdata` volume (not orphaned to anonymous volume)

Production data migration is handled separately on the Hetzner VM before this compose change is deployed.
EOF
)" 2>&1 | tail -3

content.text:
PR description trimmed to just the technical change. Now the migration plan — running through it as a dry plan first, **not executing** anything destructive without your go-ahead:

## VM-side data migration plan (Hetzner, before the compose change deploys)

The goal: move data from anonymous volume `61615080...` → named volume `gaia-prod_pgdata`, then update the live swarm service mount path. After this, when master eventually deploys the new compose, the deploy is a no-op for the volume layout because it's already correct.

### Step 0 — Safety net (already done)
- ✅ Active cluster `pg_dumpall` saved locally (`active-cluster-*.sql`, 79.7MB) — recoverable if anything goes wrong.

### Step 1 — Take a fresh dump right before migration
Catches any writes between then and migration moment.

### Step 2 — Scale postgres service down
```
ssh gaia-prod 'docker service scale gaia-prod_postgres=0'
```
Wait for the container to exit. API/workers will start erroring on DB connects — acceptable for the short migration window. The anonymous volume `61615080...` stays intact on disk.

### Step 3 — Copy data from anonymous volume into named volume
```
ssh gaia-prod 'docker run --rm \
  -v 6161508058b790aeec0db648532bd6737ae22a61a1550782c58a2d46ad7c448b:/src:ro \
  -v gaia-prod_pgdata:/dst \
  alpine sh -c "cp -a /src/. /dst/ && ls -la /dst/"'
```
After this, the named volume contains `18/docker/` cluster files (matching the structure pg18 expects when mount is at `/var/lib/postgresql/`).

### Step 4 — Update the swarm service mount path
```
ssh gaia-prod 'docker service update \
  --mount-rm /var/lib/postgresql/data \
  --mount-add type=volume,source=gaia-prod_pgdata,target=/var/lib/postgresql \
  gaia-prod_postgres'
```
This updates the swarm state. Swarm will start a new container with the new mount once we scale back up.

### Step 5 — Scale postgres back up
```
ssh gaia-prod 'docker service scale gaia-prod_postgres=1'
```
New container starts. Mount: `gaia-prod_pgdata → /var/lib/postgresql`. Pg18 finds existing cluster at `/var/lib/postgresql/18/docker/`, recognizes it (no "fresh init"), accepts connections.

### Step 6 — Verify
```
ssh gaia-prod 'docker exec $(docker ps -q -f name=gaia-prod_postgres) psql -U postgres -d langgraph -c "SELECT count(*) FROM checkpoints;"'
```
Row counts should match what was in the dump.

### Step 7 — After verifying
- API/workers reconnect automatically once postgres is healthy.
- Now you can merge the PR and deploy from master whenever you want. The deploy will be a no-op for the postgres volume layout because the swarm state already matches.

### Step 8 — Cleanup (after a few days of stable operation, for safety margin)
```
ssh gaia-prod 'docker volume prune -f'
```
This removes the 4 old orphan postgres volumes you no longer need.

## Risks / Open questions

1. **Mount-add syntax in swarm**: I'm fairly confident this works but the `--mount-add` flag on `service update` has quirks. I'd want to test the syntax against a non-prod service first, OR rehearse the same migration on the `gaia-lab` orbstack swarm (it's listed in your docker contexts as "OrbStack amd64 lab VM (mirrors gaia-prod)").

2. **Downtime**: between step 2 (scale to 0) and step 5 healthy, postgres is down. API will return 500s for DB-dependent endpoints. Total expected: ~30 seconds if `cp -a` is fast (only ~30MB to copy).

3. **PG18 version pin**: the cluster on disk says `PG_VERSION=18`. The new container will be `postgres:18-alpine` (same image, same major). No upgrade involved. Safe.

4. **If step 5 fails** (pg18 refuses to start on copied data for some reason): scale back to 0, revert mount via `docker service update --mount-rm /var/lib/postgresql --mount-add type=volume,source=gaia-prod_pgdata,target=/var/lib/postgresql/data`, scale to 1. Data may or may not be readable depending on what state it's in. Last-resort: restore the dump into a fresh cluster.

## What I'd like to do next, autonomously

- **Rehearse on `gaia-lab` first** if you want belt-and-suspenders verification. Or skip if you trust the plan.
- I will **not** run steps 2–5 on prod without your explicit go-ahead — that's the destructive-on-shared-systems line.

Want me to rehearse on `gaia-lab`? Or are you comfortable jumping straight to prod with me running each step on your green light?

3	GAIA is a proactive personal AI assistant — full-stack Nx monorepo with a Next.js frontend, FastAPI/LangGraph backend, React Native mobile app, Electron desktop app, and Discord/Slack/Telegram bots.
4	
5	## mise
6	
7	`mise` is the task runner and tool version manager for this repo. It manages Node, Python, uv, and nx versions, and defines all development tasks.
8	
9	```bash
10	mise tasks          # List all available tasks with descriptions
11	mise run <task>     # Run a task (e.g. mise run lint, mise run dev)
12	mise //apps/api:lint  # Run a task in a sub-project from the root
13	```
14	
15	Pre-commit hooks are managed via **prek** (installed by mise). Install once with `mise run pre-commit:install`. Hooks run automatically on `git commit` — to run manually: `mise run pre-commit`.
16	
17	## Key Commands
18	
19	```bash
20	# Install JS dependencies
21	pnpm install
22	
23	# Sync Python dependencies
24	nx run api:sync
25	nx run voice-agent:sync
26	
27	# Run apps
28	nx dev web          # Next.js (Turbopack)
29	nx dev api          # FastAPI (hot reload, port 8000)
30	nx worker api       # ARQ background worker
31	nx dev desktop      # Electron + Next.js
32	nx dev mobile       # React Native (Expo)
33	nx dev voice-agent  # LiveKit voice worker
34	
35	# Docker (from infra/docker/)
36	docker compose up -d                       # infra only
37	docker compose --profile backend up -d    # + API
38	docker compose --profile all up -d        # everything
39	
40	# Quality (run after changes — see After Major Changes below)
41	nx run-many -t lint
42	nx run-many -t type-check
43	nx run-many -t format
44	
45	# Build
46	nx build web
47	nx build api
48	
49	# API tests
50	cd apps/api && uv run pytest
51	```

67	## Architecture
68	
69	### Monorepo Structure
70	
71	```
72	apps/
73	  web/          - Next.js web application
74	  desktop/      - Electron desktop app
75	  mobile/       - React Native mobile app
76	  api/          - FastAPI backend with LangGraph agents
77	  voice-agent/  - Voice processing worker
78	  bots/
79	    discord/    - Discord bot
80	    slack/      - Slack bot
81	    telegram/   - Telegram bot
82	  docs/         - Documentation site
83	
84	libs/
85	  shared/       - Shared Python utilities (gaia-shared package)
86	    py/         - Python shared code
87	    ts/         - TypeScript shared code
88	```
89	
90	### Frontend (Web/Desktop)
91	
92	**Tech Stack**: Next.js 16, React 19, TypeScript, Zustand, TailwindCSS, Biome
93	
94	**Key Directories**:
95	
96	- `src/app/` - Next.js App Router pages (organized by route groups)
97	- `src/features/` - Feature modules (chat, todo, calendar, workflows, integrations, etc.)
98	- `src/stores/` - Zustand state management stores
99	- `src/components/` - Reusable React components
100	- `src/lib/` - Utility functions and configurations
101	- `src/types/` - TypeScript type definitions
102	
103	**State Management**: Uses Zustand for global state. Each feature can have its own store in `src/stores/` or `src/features/{feature}/stores/`.
104	
105	**Styling**: TailwindCSS with custom configuration. Uses Biome for linting/formatting instead of ESLint/Prettier.
106	
107	**Desktop App**: The Electron app uses the Next.js standalone build output to bundle the web app for desktop.
108	
109	### Backend (API)
110	
111	**Tech Stack**: FastAPI, LangGraph, Python 3.11+, PostgreSQL, MongoDB, Redis, ChromaDB, RabbitMQ
112	
113	**Key Directories**:
114	
115	- `app/main.py` - Application entry point
116	- `app/core/` - Core application logic (app factory, middleware, lifespan)
117	- `app/api/v1/` - API routes and endpoints
118	- `app/agents/` - LangGraph agent system
119	  - `core/` - Core agent logic (agent.py, state.py, graph_manager.py, nodes/, subagents/)
120	  - `tools/` - Agent tools
121	  - `prompts/` - Agent prompts
122	  - `memory/` - Agent memory management
123	  - `llm/` - LLM integrations
124	- `app/models/` - Database models
125	- `app/schemas/` - Pydantic schemas
126	- `app/services/` - Business logic services
127	- `app/db/` - Database clients (postgresql, mongodb, redis, chroma, rabbitmq)
128	- `app/workers/` - Background task workers
129	- `app/config/` - Configuration and settings
130	- `app/utils/` - Utility functions
131	
132	**Database Setup**: The API depends on PostgreSQL, MongoDB, Redis, ChromaDB, and RabbitMQ. Use Docker Compose for local development.
133	
134	**Background Tasks**: Uses ARQ (Redis-based task queue) for async job processing. Run with `nx worker api`.
135	
136	**Dependency Management**: Uses `uv` for Python package management. Run `nx run api:sync` to install dependencies.
137	
138	### Mobile App
139	
140	**Tech Stack**: React Native, Expo, TypeScript
141	
142	Similar structure to web app with React Native components. Uses React Navigation for routing.
143	
144	### Shared Libraries
145	
146	**Python Shared (`libs/shared/`)**: Common utilities used across Python apps (API, voice-agent, bots). Includes logging, config, and Pydantic models.
147	
148	**Install**: The `gaia-shared` package is automatically available to Python apps via workspace dependencies.
149	
150	## Design System
151	
152	The full design system is documented in **[`DESIGN.md`](./DESIGN.md)** at the repo root. It covers:
153	- Color tokens, zinc scale, semantic status colors, dark/light CSS variables
154	- Typography (Inter, PP Editorial New, Anonymous Pro) and heading scale
155	- Spacing, border radius decision table, shadows
156	- Icon library usage (`@icons` — never raw SVGs)
157	- Animation tokens, Framer Motion conventions, easing functions
158	- Toast/notification system (Sileo — never sonner or react-hot-toast)
159	- Chat bubble architecture and the TextBubble/TOOL_RENDERERS system
160	- **Chat tool card styling contract** (outer `rounded-2xl bg-zinc-800 p-4`, inner `rounded-2xl bg-zinc-900 p-3`, no borders)
161	- Adding new tool cards vs OpenUI primitives (decision tree)
162	- Copy-paste card template and pre-commit checklist
163	
164	**Claude rules** for design consistency are in `.claude/rules/design.md` (auto-loaded).
165	**Chat bubble rules** are in `apps/web/src/features/chat/components/bubbles/bot/CLAUDE.md`.
166	**Visual style guide** (rendered as interactive docs) is in `docs/design-system.mdx` — sourced from `DESIGN.md`.
167	
168	### Component Library — Never Build From Scratch
169	
170	**Never create custom button, input, spinner, tooltip, modal, or other UI primitive components from scratch.** Always use HeroUI first:
171	
172	- `<Button>` — never `<button>`. Use `color`, `variant`, `radius`, `size`, `endContent`, `startContent`, `isLoading`, `isIconOnly` props.
173	- `<Input>` / `<Textarea>` — never raw `<input>` / `<textarea>`
174	- `<Spinner>` / `<Skeleton>` — never custom loaders or icon-based spinners
175	- `<Tooltip>`, `<Popover>`, `<Modal>`, `<Dropdown>` — never custom implementations
176	- `<Link>` — never `<a>` tags (use HeroUI or Next.js Link)
177	- `<Chip>` — for status badges and tags
178	- `<Divider>` — never `<hr>`
179	
180	If HeroUI doesn't cover the use case, reach for Shadcn/Radix. Only build a custom component when no library equivalent exists.
181	
182	## Code Style
183	
184	### TypeScript/JavaScript
185	
186	- Package manager is **pnpm** — never use npm or yarn
187	- **Biome** for linting/formatting — not ESLint/Prettier
188	- **No inline imports** — all imports at the top of the file
189	- **Never use `any`** — always provide proper type definitions
190	- **Before creating a new type, search `src/types/` first** — do not duplicate existing types
191	- Path alias `@/` maps to `src/` in web/desktop
192	- **Never use Unicode/text symbols as UI elements** — no `→`, `↗`, `←`, `↑`, `↓`, `•`, `✓`, `×`, or any other Unicode symbol characters in rendered JSX. Always use icon components from `@icons` instead. This applies everywhere: demo components, cards, labels, badges, list items.
193

56	
57	## Profiles (docker-compose.yml)
58	
59	Services gated by profile (not started by default):
60	- `backend` — `gaia-backend`, `voice-agent-worker`
61	- `worker` — `arq_worker`
62	- `voice` — `voice-agent-worker`
63	- `bots` — `discord-bot`, `slack-bot`, `telegram-bot`, `whatsapp-bot`
64	- `observability` — `loki`, `promtail`, `grafana`
65	- `all` — everything above
66	
67	Infrastructure services (postgres, redis, mongo, chromadb, rabbitmq) start without any profile. Observability stack (loki/promtail/grafana) is opt-in — set `COMPOSE_PROFILES=observability` in `infra/docker/.env` or run `docker compose --profile observability up -d`.
68	
69	## Service Dependencies
70	
71	```
72	gaia-backend → chromadb, postgres, redis, mongo (all healthy)
73	arq_worker   → redis, mongo (healthy)
74	bots         → gaia-backend (healthy)
75	voice-agent  → (no hard deps in dev; depends on gaia-backend in prod)

1	name: gaia-dev
2	
3	services:
4	  # FastAPI Application (use profile to run in docker, otherwise run locally with mise)
5	  gaia-backend:
6	    container_name: gaia-backend
7	    profiles: ["backend", "all"]
8	    build:
9	      context: ../..
10	      dockerfile: apps/api/Dockerfile
11	    image: gaia
12	    working_dir: /app/apps/api
13	    command:
14	      [
15	        "uv",
16	        "run",
17	        "python",
18	        "-m",
19	        "uvicorn",
20	        "app.main:app",
21	        "--host",
22	        "0.0.0.0",
23	        "--port",
24	        "80",
25	        "--reload",
26	        "--no-access-log",
27	      ]
28	    environment:
29	      DEBUG: 1
30	      PYTHONUNBUFFERED: 1
31	      WATCHFILES_FORCE_POLLING: 1
32	      # PYTHONPROFILEIMPORTTIME: 1
33	      FORCE_COLOR: 0
34	      WORKER_TYPE: main_app
35	      PYTHONPATH: /app/apps/api
36	      LOG_FORMAT: json
37	      LOG_COLORIZE: "false"
38	    env_file:
39	      - ../../apps/api/.env
40	    volumes:
41	      - ../../apps/api/app:/app/apps/api/app
42	    ports:
43	      - "${API_HOST_PORT:-8000}:80"
44	    restart: on-failure
45	    tty: true
46	    stdin_open: true

49	        condition: service_healthy

51	        condition: service_healthy

83	    networks:

attachment.content:
- content-research-writer: Assists in writing high-quality content by conducting research, adding citations, improving hooks, iterating on outlines, and providing real-time feedback on each section. Transforms your writing process from solo effort to collaborative partnership.
- hyperframes: Create video compositions, animations, title cards, overlays, captions, voiceovers, audio-reactive visuals, and scene transitions in HyperFrames HTML. Use when asked to build any HTML-based video content, add captions or subtitles synced to audio, generate text-to-speech narration, create audio-reactive animation (beat sync, glow, pulse driven by music), add animated text highlighting (marker sweeps, hand-drawn circles, burst lines, scribble, sketchout), or add transitions between scenes (crossfades, wipes, reveals, shader transitions). Covers composition authoring, timing, media, and the full video production workflow. For CLI commands (init, lint, preview, render, transcribe, tts) see the hyperframes-cli skill.
- website-to-hyperframes: Capture a website and create a HyperFrames video from it. Use when: (1) a user provides a URL and wants a video, (2) someone says "capture this site", "turn this into a video", "make a promo from my site", (3) the user wants a social ad, product tour, or any video based on an existing website, (4) the user shares a link and asks for any kind of video content. Even if the user just pastes a URL — this is the skill to use.
- commit: Create commit messages following Sentry conventions. Use when committing code changes, writing commit messages, or formatting git history. Follows conventional commits with Sentry-specific issue references.
- remotion-to-hyperframes: Translate an existing Remotion (React-based) video composition into a HyperFrames HTML composition. Use ONLY when the user explicitly asks to port, convert, migrate, translate, or rewrite a Remotion composition as HyperFrames — for example "port my Remotion project to HyperFrames", "convert this Remotion code to HyperFrames", "migrate from Remotion", "translate this Remotion comp", or "rewrite this as HyperFrames HTML". Do NOT use when (a) the user is authoring a NEW HyperFrames composition, even if they have or are A/B-testing a similar Remotion video; (b) the user mentions Remotion in passing without asking for migration; (c) the user shares Remotion code as reference material rather than asking for a translation; (d) the user asks for "the same video as my Remotion one" without explicitly asking to migrate the source — treat that as a fresh HyperFrames build. When in doubt, default to authoring a native HyperFrames composition with the `hyperframes` skill instead. Skill detects unsupported patterns (useState, useEffect with side effects, async calculateMetadata, third-party React component libraries, `@remotion/lambda` features) and recommends the runtime interop escape hatch instead of attempting a lossy translation.
- hyperframes-cli: HyperFrames CLI tool — hyperframes init, lint, inspect, preview, render, transcribe, tts, doctor, browser, info, upgrade, compositions, docs, benchmark. Use when scaffolding a project, linting, validating, inspecting visual layout in compositions, previewing in the studio, rendering to video, transcribing audio, generating TTS, or troubleshooting the HyperFrames environment.
- using-git-worktrees: Use when starting feature work that needs isolation from current workspace or before executing implementation plans - creates isolated git worktrees with smart directory selection and safety verification
- gaia-video-animations: Create high-quality Remotion video animations for GAIA product commercials. Use when building, modifying, or creating new scenes for the GAIA promotional video (apps/video), creating Apple-style motion design commercials in Remotion, or when working on any video animation in the GAIA monorepo. Covers scene architecture, animation patterns, typography, transitions, sound design, and aesthetic rules derived from 72 production conversations. Also use when the user asks to create a new video, add scenes, fix animation timing, or improve visual quality of Remotion compositions.
- langsmith-fetch: Debug LangChain and LangGraph agents by fetching execution traces from LangSmith Studio. Use when debugging agent behavior, investigating errors, analyzing tool calls, checking memory operations, or examining agent performance. Automatically fetches recent traces and analyzes execution patterns. Requires langsmith-fetch CLI installed.
- remotion-best-practices: Best practices for Remotion - Video creation in React
- template-skill: Replace with description of the skill and when Claude should use it.
- waapi: Web Animations API adapter patterns for HyperFrames. Use when authoring element.animate() motion, Animation currentTime seeking, document.getAnimations(), KeyframeEffect timing, fill modes, or native browser animations that must render deterministically in HyperFrames.
- systematic-debugging: Use when encountering any bug, test failure, or unexpected behavior, before proposing fixes
- terminal-title: Automatically updates terminal window title to reflect the current high-level task. Use at the start of every Claude Code session when the user provides their first prompt, and whenever the user switches to a distinctly new high-level task. content.text:
The swarm rehearsal reproduces the SAME pg18 tripwire — task keeps failing & restarting because the broken mount config + empty named volume = "data in unused mount" error. That's the same state local dev hits.

For the migration rehearsal I actually need to test:
1. `docker service update --mount-rm --mount-add` syntax (the part I'm most uncertain about)
2. Volume contents survive the swap

Let me clean up the failing pg service and test those exact mechanics with a populated postgres:

input.command:
orbctl run -m swarm-rehearsal bash -c '
sudo docker service rm pg 2>&1
sleep 3
# Step 1: get a working postgres with data using the PGDATA workaround.
# This mimics what prod looks like — a healthy pg18 with data in volume.
sudo docker service create --name pg \
  --mount type=volume,source=anon-data,target=/var/lib/postgresql \
  --mount type=volume,source=pgdata-named,target=/var/lib/postgresql/data \
  --env POSTGRES_USER=postgres --env POSTGRES_PASSWORD=postgres --env POSTGRES_DB=langgraph \
  postgres:18-alpine 2>&1 | tail -3
echo "---"
sleep 8
sudo docker service ls --filter name=pg
echo "---PS---"
sudo docker service ps pg --no-trunc --format "{{.CurrentState}} {{.Error}}" | head -3
' 2>&1 | tail -20

content.text: Let me take a more focused approach — instead of fighting swarm, prove the core migration mechanics work: volume copy + postgres starts cleanly on new mount path with copied data.

input.command:
orbctl run -m swarm-rehearsal sudo bash -c '
set -e
echo "=== Step 0: Clean slate ==="
docker service rm pg 2>/dev/null || true
sleep 2
docker volume rm anon-data pgdata-named pgdata src dst 2>/dev/null || true

echo ""
echo "=== Step 1: Start postgres healthy WITH FIXED MOUNT (mimics what prod will look like after migration) ==="
docker service create --name pg \
  --mount type=volume,source=src,target=/var/lib/postgresql \
  --env POSTGRES_PASSWORD=postgres \
  --env POSTGRES_DB=langgraph \
  postgres:18-alpine >/dev/null 2>&1

# wait for ready
for i in $(seq 1 30); do
  if docker exec $(docker ps -q -f name=pg) pg_isready -U postgres 2>/dev/null | grep -q "accepting"; then
    echo "  pg ready after ${i}*3s"
    break
  fi
  sleep 3
done

echo ""
echo "=== Step 2: Insert test data ==="
docker exec $(docker ps -q -f name=pg) psql -U postgres -d langgraph -c "CREATE TABLE rehearsal_marker(id int PRIMARY KEY, note text); INSERT INTO rehearsal_marker VALUES (1, '"'"'before-migration'"'"'), (2, '"'"'live-data'"'"');"

echo ""
echo "=== Step 3: Snapshot what we have ==="
docker exec $(docker ps -q -f name=pg) psql -U postgres -d langgraph -c "SELECT * FROM rehearsal_marker;"
echo "Volume contents:"
docker run --rm -v src:/v alpine sh -c "ls /v && cat /v/18/docker/PG_VERSION"
' 2>&1 | tail -40
input.description: Step 0-3: Get healthy postgres with test data on FIXED mount

content.text: Postgres healthy, 2 rows inserted. PG_VERSION=18 in the volume. Now testing the critical migration commands:

input.command:
orbctl run -m swarm-rehearsal sudo bash -c '
set -e

echo "=== Step 4: Scale postgres to 0 (simulates prod migration step 2) ==="
docker service scale pg=0
sleep 5
echo "Replicas now: $(docker service ls --filter name=pg --format "{{.Replicas}}")"

echo ""
echo "=== Step 5: Copy data from src volume → dst volume (simulates anon → named copy) ==="
docker run --rm -v src:/from:ro -v dst:/to alpine sh -c "cp -a /from/. /to/ && echo COPY_OK && ls /to/"

echo ""
echo "=== Step 6: docker service update --mount-rm + --mount-add (THE CRITICAL SYNTAX) ==="
docker service update \
  --mount-rm /var/lib/postgresql \
  --mount-add type=volume,source=dst,target=/var/lib/postgresql \
  pg
echo "  update exit code: $?"

echo ""
echo "=== Step 7: Scale postgres back to 1 ==="
docker service scale pg=1
for i in $(seq 1 30); do
  if docker exec $(docker ps -q -f name=pg) pg_isready -U postgres 2>/dev/null | grep -q "accepting"; then
    echo "  pg ready after ${i}*3s"
    break
  fi
  sleep 3
done

echo ""
echo "=== Step 8: Verify data persisted across the volume swap ==="
docker exec $(docker ps -q -f name=pg) psql -U postgres -d langgraph -c "SELECT * FROM rehearsal_marker;"

echo ""
echo "=== Step 9: Inspect mounts on the new container ==="
docker inspect $(docker ps -q -f name=pg) --format "{{json .Mounts}}" | python3 -m json.tool 2>/dev/null || docker inspect $(docker ps -q -f name=pg) --format "{{json .Mounts}}"
' 2>&1 | tail -60
input.description: Run the full migration sequence: scale-down, copy, mount-swap, scale-up, verify

input.command:
orbctl run -m swarm-rehearsal sudo bash -c '
set -e
echo "=== Reset: tear down service and remove volumes ==="
docker service rm pg 2>/dev/null || true
sleep 3
docker volume rm src dst 2>/dev/null
docker volume ls --format "{{.Name}}"

echo ""
echo "=== Step A: Create empty named volumes ==="
docker volume create src >/dev/null
docker volume create dst >/dev/null

echo ""
echo "=== Step B: Start pg with src mounted ==="
docker service create --name pg \
  --mount type=volume,source=src,target=/var/lib/postgresql \
  --env POSTGRES_PASSWORD=postgres --env POSTGRES_DB=langgraph \
  postgres:18-alpine >/dev/null
for i in $(seq 1 30); do
  cid=$(docker ps -q -f name=pg.1)
  if [ -n "$cid" ] && docker exec $cid pg_isready -U postgres 2>/dev/null | grep -q "accepting"; then
    echo "  ready"; break
  fi
  sleep 3
done

echo ""
echo "=== Step C: Insert test data ==="
docker exec $(docker ps -q -f name=pg.1) psql -U postgres -d langgraph -c "CREATE TABLE marker(n int); INSERT INTO marker VALUES (42), (43), (44);"

echo ""
echo "=== Step D: Scale to 0 ==="
docker service scale pg=0 --detach
for i in $(seq 1 20); do
  [ -z "$(docker ps -q -f name=pg.1)" ] && echo "  stopped" && break
  sleep 2
done

echo ""
echo "=== Step E: Inspect src volume BEFORE copy ==="
docker run --rm -v src:/v alpine sh -c "ls /v/18/docker/PG_VERSION && cat /v/18/docker/PG_VERSION"

echo ""
echo "=== Step F: Inspect dst volume BEFORE copy (should be empty) ==="
docker run --rm -v dst:/v alpine sh -c "ls -la /v/"

echo ""
echo "=== Step G: COPY src → dst ==="
docker run --rm -v src:/from:ro -v dst:/to alpine sh -c "cp -a /from/. /to/ && echo COPY_OK"

echo ""
echo "=== Step H: Inspect dst AFTER copy (should match src) ==="
docker run --rm -v dst:/v alpine sh -c "ls /v/ && ls /v/18/docker/PG_VERSION && cat /v/18/docker/PG_VERSION"
' 2>&1 | tail -50

input.command:
orbctl run -m swarm-rehearsal sudo bash -c '
set -e
echo "=== Step I: Service update — change mount from src to dst ==="
docker service update --mount-rm /var/lib/postgresql --mount-add type=volume,source=dst,target=/var/lib/postgresql pg --detach 2>&1 | tail -3

echo ""
echo "=== Step J: Inspect service spec AFTER update ==="
docker service inspect pg --format "{{json .Spec.TaskTemplate.ContainerSpec.Mounts}}"

echo ""
echo "=== Step K: Scale to 1 ==="
docker service scale pg=1 --detach
for i in $(seq 1 30); do
  cid=$(docker ps -q -f name=pg.1)
  if [ -n "$cid" ] && docker exec $cid pg_isready -U postgres 2>/dev/null | grep -q "accepting"; then
    echo "  ready after ${i}*3s"; break
  fi
  sleep 3
done

echo ""
echo "=== Step L: Check what pg sees ==="
docker exec $(docker ps -q -f name=pg.1) ls /var/lib/postgresql/18/docker/PG_VERSION
docker exec $(docker ps -q -f name=pg.1) cat /var/lib/postgresql/18/docker/PG_VERSION

echo ""
echo "=== Step M: Check pg logs for skip-init vs init message ==="
docker service logs pg --since 1m 2>&1 | grep -iE "skipping|init process complete|database system|ready to accept" | tail -10

echo ""
echo "=== Step N: Query the marker table ==="
docker exec $(docker ps -q -f name=pg.1) psql -U postgres -d langgraph -c "SELECT * FROM marker;"
' 2>&1 | tail -40

content.text:
**Found the bug.** Step J shows mounts are `null` after the update — `--mount-add` silently failed (probably command parsing) and only `--mount-rm` took effect. New postgres started with no volume → init'd fresh cluster → all data gone.

Let me find the correct `--mount-add` syntax:

input.command:
orbctl run -m swarm-rehearsal sudo bash -c '
set -e
echo "=== Test: add a mount via update (clean state) ==="
docker service rm pg 2>/dev/null || true
sleep 2
docker volume rm dst 2>/dev/null
docker volume create dst >/dev/null

# Pre-populate dst with data (copy from src which still has table)
docker run --rm -v src:/from:ro -v dst:/to alpine cp -a /from/. /to/ >/dev/null
docker run --rm -v dst:/v alpine cat /v/18/docker/PG_VERSION

# Create a fresh service WITHOUT mounts
docker service create --name pg --env POSTGRES_PASSWORD=postgres --env POSTGRES_DB=langgraph postgres:18-alpine --replicas 0 >/dev/null 2>&1 || \
docker service create --name pg --env POSTGRES_PASSWORD=postgres --env POSTGRES_DB=langgraph --replicas 0 postgres:18-alpine >/dev/null
sleep 2

echo ""
echo "=== Try --mount-add with type=volume,source=,target= syntax ==="
docker service update --mount-add "type=volume,source=dst,target=/var/lib/postgresql" pg --detach 2>&1 | tail -2

echo ""
echo "=== Inspect mounts after add ==="
docker service inspect pg --format "{{json .Spec.TaskTemplate.ContainerSpec.Mounts}}"
' 2>&1 | tail -25

content.content:

pg
dst
18

=== Try --mount-add with type=volume,source=,target= syntax ===
pg

=== Inspect mounts after add ===
[{"Type":"volume","Source":"dst","Target":"/var/lib/postgresql"}]

dst

aiTitle: Fix PostgreSQL Docker volume configuration for version 18

content.text: Quoted `--mount-add` works. The combined `--mount-rm` + `--mount-add` failed because in my heredoc the comma-separated value wasn't quoted. Let me retest properly:

input.command:
orbctl run -m swarm-rehearsal sudo bash -c '
set -e
echo "=== Reset to original working state: pg with src mounted, scaled up ==="
docker service rm pg 2>/dev/null || true
sleep 3
docker service create --name pg \
  --mount type=volume,source=src,target=/var/lib/postgresql \
  --env POSTGRES_PASSWORD=postgres --env POSTGRES_DB=langgraph \
  postgres:18-alpine >/dev/null
for i in $(seq 1 30); do
  cid=$(docker ps -q -f name=pg.1)
  if [ -n "$cid" ] && docker exec $cid pg_isready -U postgres 2>/dev/null | grep -q "accepting"; then
    echo "  ready"; break
  fi
  sleep 3
done
docker exec $(docker ps -q -f name=pg.1) psql -U postgres -d langgraph -c "SELECT * FROM marker;" 2>&1 | head -10

echo ""
echo "=== Verify marker table exists in src ==="

echo ""
echo "=== Step D: Scale to 0 ==="
docker service scale pg=0 --detach
for i in $(seq 1 20); do
  [ -z "$(docker ps -q -f name=pg.1)" ] && echo "  stopped" && break
  sleep 2
done

echo ""
echo "=== Step G: Recreate dst empty and copy src → dst ==="
docker volume rm dst >/dev/null 2>&1; docker volume create dst >/dev/null
docker run --rm -v src:/from:ro -v dst:/to alpine sh -c "cp -a /from/. /to/ && cat /to/18/docker/PG_VERSION"

echo ""
echo "=== Step I: Service update — mount-rm AND mount-add together, WITH QUOTES ==="
docker service update \
  --mount-rm /var/lib/postgresql \
  --mount-add "type=volume,source=dst,target=/var/lib/postgresql" \
  --detach \
  pg 2>&1 | tail -2

echo ""
echo "=== Step J: Mounts after update ==="
docker service inspect pg --format "{{json .Spec.TaskTemplate.ContainerSpec.Mounts}}"

echo ""
echo "=== Step K: Scale to 1 ==="
docker service scale pg=1 --detach
for i in $(seq 1 30); do
  cid=$(docker ps -q -f name=pg.1)
  if [ -n "$cid" ] && docker exec $cid pg_isready -U postgres 2>/dev/null | grep -q "accepting"; then
    echo "  ready"; break
  fi
  sleep 3
done

echo ""
echo "=== Step N: QUERY the marker table from new postgres ==="
docker exec $(docker ps -q -f name=pg.1) psql -U postgres -d langgraph -c "SELECT * FROM marker;"

echo ""
echo "=== Step O: Check logs for skip vs init ==="
docker service logs pg --since 30s 2>&1 | grep -iE "skipping init|init process complete|shutdown" | tail -5
' 2>&1 | tail -50
input.description: Full migration sequence with correctly quoted mount-add

aiTitle: Fix PostgreSQL Docker volume configuration for version 18

content.text: Combined `--mount-rm` + `--mount-add` in one command DOES NOT WORK in this docker version — even with quoting, mounts end up null. Need to do them as two separate updates:

input.command:
orbctl run -m swarm-rehearsal sudo bash -c '
set -e
echo "=== Reset clean ==="
docker service rm pg 2>/dev/null || true
sleep 3
docker volume rm src dst 2>/dev/null
docker volume create src >/dev/null

# Start with src
docker service create --name pg \
  --mount type=volume,source=src,target=/var/lib/postgresql \
  --env POSTGRES_PASSWORD=postgres --env POSTGRES_DB=langgraph \
  postgres:18-alpine >/dev/null
for i in $(seq 1 30); do
  cid=$(docker ps -q -f name=pg.1)
  if [ -n "$cid" ] && docker exec $cid pg_isready -U postgres 2>/dev/null | grep -q "accepting"; then break; fi
  sleep 3
done
docker exec $(docker ps -q -f name=pg.1) psql -U postgres -d langgraph -c "CREATE TABLE marker(n int); INSERT INTO marker VALUES (42), (43), (44);" >/dev/null
echo "  marker table created"

echo ""
echo "=== Scale to 0 ==="
docker service scale pg=0 --detach
for i in $(seq 1 20); do
  [ -z "$(docker ps -q -f name=pg.1)" ] && break
  sleep 2
done

echo ""
echo "=== Copy src → dst ==="
docker volume create dst >/dev/null
docker run --rm -v src:/from:ro -v dst:/to alpine cp -a /from/. /to/

echo ""
echo "=== TWO SEPARATE service update calls ==="
echo "--- First: --mount-rm ---"
docker service update --mount-rm /var/lib/postgresql --detach pg
echo "  mounts after rm:"
docker service inspect pg --format "{{json .Spec.TaskTemplate.ContainerSpec.Mounts}}"

echo "--- Second: --mount-add ---"
docker service update --mount-add "type=volume,source=dst,target=/var/lib/postgresql" --detach pg
echo "  mounts after add:"
docker service inspect pg --format "{{json .Spec.TaskTemplate.ContainerSpec.Mounts}}"

echo ""
echo "=== Scale to 1 ==="
docker service scale pg=1 --detach
for i in $(seq 1 30); do
  cid=$(docker ps -q -f name=pg.1)
  if [ -n "$cid" ] && docker exec $cid pg_isready -U postgres 2>/dev/null | grep -q "accepting"; then break; fi
  sleep 3
done

echo ""
echo "=== Query marker table ==="
docker exec $(docker ps -q -f name=pg.1) psql -U postgres -d langgraph -c "SELECT * FROM marker;"

echo ""
echo "=== Recent pg logs ==="
docker service logs pg --since 1m 2>&1 | grep -iE "skipping|init process|database system was|ready to accept" | tail -5
' 2>&1 | tail -40
input.description: Two separate service update calls for mount-rm then mount-add

aiTitle: Fix PostgreSQL Docker volume configuration for version 18

content.text:
Lab cleaned up. Here's the rehearsal report:

## Lab rehearsal report

**Setup:** OrbStack amd64 Ubuntu VM with Docker 29.1.3 + single-node swarm. Postgres:18-alpine deployed with named volume `src` at `/var/lib/postgresql`. Created `marker` table with 3 rows, then ran through the full migration sequence.

### What worked ✅

- **Volume-to-volume copy via throwaway alpine container** — `docker run --rm -v src:/from:ro -v dst:/to alpine cp -a /from/. /to/` cleanly mirrored 18/docker/ cluster files into the new volume.
- **`docker service scale pg=0`** to stop the service cleanly. Container stopped, named volume contents intact.
- **`docker service update --mount-add "type=volume,source=...,target=..."`** with the comma-separated value quoted — the new mount appears in the service spec.
- **Postgres recognized existing cluster on new mount** — log line "PostgreSQL Database directory appears to contain a database; Skipping initialization" confirmed. **Data fully preserved** — all rows queryable after restart.

### What DIDN'T work ⚠️

**This is the gotcha and would have nuked prod data if I'd done it the way my plan said.**

```bash
# DOES NOT WORK — ends with mounts == null
docker service update \
  --mount-rm /var/lib/postgresql \
  --mount-add "type=volume,source=dst,target=/var/lib/postgresql" \
  pg
```

In Docker 29.1.3, combining `--mount-rm` and `--mount-add` in a **single** `docker service update` call drops the mount entirely. The `--mount-rm` removes it, but the `--mount-add` is silently ignored — service spec ends up with `null` mounts. Container then starts with no volume → fresh empty cluster → all data gone.

**Fix:** run them as **two separate `docker service update` calls**:

```bash
# Correct sequence
docker --context prod service update --mount-rm /var/lib/postgresql/data --detach gaia-prod_postgres
docker --context prod service update --mount-add "type=volume,source=gaia-prod_pgdata,target=/var/lib/postgresql" --detach gaia-prod_postgres
```

After the second update, `docker service inspect` confirms the mount, then `service scale ... =1` and postgres comes up reading the existing cluster.

### Other minor learnings

- `--mount-add` value MUST be quoted (the comma confuses arg parsing in some contexts).
- The image's `VOLUME /var/lib/postgresql` directive means Docker auto-creates an anonymous volume there if you don't explicitly mount one (this is the original bug on prod). When we explicitly mount our named volume at that path, no phantom volume is created.
- Default `PGDATA=/var/lib/postgresql/18/docker` in pg18-alpine — confirmed via `docker run --rm postgres:18-alpine env | grep PGDATA`.

### Updated prod migration sequence (the safe one)

```bash
# 0. Fresh dump as safety net
ssh gaia-prod 'docker exec $(docker ps -q -f name=gaia-prod_postgres) pg_dumpall -U postgres --clean --if-exists' > final-prod-dump.sql

# 1. Scale postgres service to 0
docker --context prod service scale gaia-prod_postgres=0

# 2. Wait for container exit, then copy anon vol → named vol
ssh gaia-prod 'docker run --rm \
  -v 6161508058b790aeec0db648532bd6737ae22a61a1550782c58a2d46ad7c448b:/from:ro \
  -v gaia-prod_pgdata:/to \
  alpine cp -a /from/. /to/'

# 3. TWO SEPARATE update calls — DO NOT combine
docker --context prod service update --mount-rm /var/lib/postgresql/data --detach gaia-prod_postgres
docker --context prod service update --mount-add "type=volume,source=gaia-prod_pgdata,target=/var/lib/postgresql" --detach gaia-prod_postgres

# 4. Verify mount appears
docker --context prod service inspect gaia-prod_postgres --format '{{json .Spec.TaskTemplate.ContainerSpec.Mounts}}'

# 5. Scale back up
docker --context prod service scale gaia-prod_postgres=1

# 6. Verify data
ssh gaia-prod 'docker exec $(docker ps -q -f name=gaia-prod_postgres) psql -U postgres -d langgraph -c "SELECT count(*) FROM checkpoints;"'
```

Lab rehearsal proved this sequence preserves data end-to-end. Ready when you are — just say "go" and I'll execute it on prod step by step, pausing after each command for verification.

- emilkowal-animations: Emil Kowalski's animation best practices for web interfaces. Use when writing, reviewing, or implementing animations in React, CSS, or Framer Motion. Triggers on tasks involving transitions, easing, gestures, toasts, drawers, or motion.
- overdrive: Pushes interfaces past conventional limits with technically ambitious implementations — shaders, spring physics, scroll-driven reveals, 60fps animations. Use when the user wants to wow, impress, go all-out, or make something that feels extraordinary.
- do-linear-issues: Implement multiple Linear issues in parallel — each in its own worktree, branch, and PR off develop
- rams: Run accessibility and visual design review
- verify: Run full quality gates before marking work done. Stop and report any failures — do not mark work ...

- vercel:env: Manage Vercel environment variables. Commands include list, pull, add, remove, and diff. Use to sync environment variables between Vercel and your local development environment.
- vercel:marketplace: Discover and install Vercel Marketplace integrations. Use to find databases, CMS, auth providers, and other services available on the Vercel Marketplace.
- vercel:status: Show the status of the current Vercel project — recent deployments, linked project info, and environment overview.

- vercel:vercel-agent: Vercel Agent guidance — AI-powered code review, incident investigation, and SDK installation. Automates PR analysis and anomaly debugging. Use when configuring or understanding Vercel's AI development tools.
- vercel:verification: Full-story verification — infers what the user is building, then verifies the complete flow end-to-end: browser → API → data → response. Triggers on dev server start and 'why isn't this working' signals.
- vercel:workflow: Vercel Workflow DevKit (WDK) expert guidance. Use when building durable workflows, long-running tasks, API routes or agents that need pause/resume, retries, step-based execution, or crash-safe orchestration with Vercel Workflow.
- vercel:ai-sdk: Vercel AI SDK expert guidance. Use when building AI-powered features — chat interfaces, text generation, structured output, tool calling, agents, MCP integration, streaming, embeddings, reranking, image generation, or working with any LLM provider.
- vercel:vercel-functions: Vercel Functions expert guidance — Serverless Functions, Edge Functions, Fluid Compute, streaming, Cron Jobs, and runtime configuration. Use when configuring, debugging, or optimizing server-side code running on Vercel.
- vercel:vercel-cli: Vercel CLI expert guidance. Use when deploying, managing environment variables, linking projects, viewing logs, querying metrics, managing domains, or interacting with the Vercel platform from the command line.
- vercel:chat-sdk: Vercel Chat SDK expert guidance. Use when building multi-platform chat bots — Slack, Telegram, Microsoft Teams, Discord, Google Chat, GitHub, Linear — with a single codebase. Covers the Chat class, adapters, threads, messages, cards, modals, streaming, state management, and webhook setup.
- vercel:vercel-sandbox: Vercel Sandbox guidance — ephemeral Firecracker microVMs for running untrusted code safely. Supports AI agents, code generation, and experimentation. Use when executing user-generated or AI-generated code in isolation.
- vercel:react-best-practices: React best-practices reviewer for TSX files. Triggers after editing multiple TSX components to run a condensed quality checklist covering component structure, hooks usage, accessibility, performance, and TypeScript patterns.
- vercel:next-forge: next-forge expert guidance — production-grade Turborepo monorepo SaaS starter by Vercel. Use when working in a next-forge project, scaffolding with `npx next-forge init`, or editing @repo/* workspace packages.
- vercel:next-upgrade: Upgrade Next.js to the latest version following official migration guides and codemods. Use when upgrading Next.js versions, running codemods, or migrating between major releases.
- vercel:turbopack: Turbopack expert guidance. Use when configuring the Next.js bundler, optimizing HMR, debugging build issues, or understanding the Turbopack vs Webpack differences.
- vercel:env-vars: Vercel environment variable expert guidance. Use when working with .env files, vercel env commands, OIDC tokens, or managing environment-specific configuration.
- vercel:vercel-storage: Vercel storage expert guidance — Blob, Edge Config, and Marketplace storage (Neon Postgres, Upstash Redis). Use when choosing, configuring, or using data storage with Vercel applications.
- vercel:next-cache-components: Next.js 16 Cache Components guidance — PPR, use cache directive, cacheLife, cacheTag, updateTag, and migration from unstable_cache. Use when implementing partial prerendering, caching strategies, or migrating from older Next.js cache patterns.
- vercel:deployments-cicd: Vercel deployment and CI/CD expert guidance. Use when deploying, promoting, rolling back, inspecting deployments, building with --prebuilt, or configuring CI workflow files for Vercel.

Use when initializing shadcn, adding components, composing product UI, building custom registries, configuring themes, or troubleshooting component issues.
- vercel:knowledge-update: Corrects outdated LLM knowledge about the Vercel platform and introduces new products. Injected at session start.

- vercel:routing-middleware: Vercel Routing Middleware guidance — request interception before cache, rewrites, redirects, personalization. Works with any framework. Supports Edge, Node.js, and Bun runtimes. Use when intercepting requests at the platform level.

- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init: Initialize new CLAUDE.md file(s) and optional skills/hooks with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review

aiTitle: Fix PostgreSQL Docker volume configuration for version 18

input.command:
echo "=== Lab VMs ==="; orbctl list 2>&1
echo ""
echo "=== Prod sidecars ==="; ssh gaia-prod 'docker ps -a --filter name=pg-orphan --format "{{.Names}} {{.Status}}"'
echo ""
echo "=== Local dump files ==="; ls -lh ~/gaia-prod-pg-rescue/
echo ""
echo "=== Local docker compose state (onboarding) ==="; docker ps --filter name=postgres --format '{{.Names}} {{.Status}}'
echo ""
echo "=== PR ==="; gh pr view 681 --json state,title,url --jq '.'

**Still pending — needs your green light:**
1. **Prod migration** — run the 6-step sequence from the rehearsal report (scale=0 → copy anon→named → 2 separate mount updates → scale=1 → verify)

Nothing else is running or in flight. Ready when you are.
